reasoning: The prediction concerns the 1-week change in the GBP/USD exchange rate from March 15 to March 22, 2026. Exchange rate movements over short horizons generally approximate a random walk, providing a strong prior of 0.50. Recent FRED data shows a sharp drop in GBP/USD to 1.324 on March 13, pushing it well below the 200-day SMA (1.3415) and the institutional support zone of 1.33-1.34. Both the FOMC and the Bank of England hold policy meetings on March 17-18, introducing high volatility during this exact window. While recent downward momentum could persist, the extreme deviation from technical support and institutional forecasts (which target 1.35-1.39) creates a slight probability skew toward mean reversion (a bounce backward/upward). The FRED linear model estimates a 52% probability of the rate exceeding the March 13 baseline. Balancing the random walk prior with the potential for mean reversion from an oversold level, I give a slight edge to an increase over the next week.
